MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-17-2019 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO, DONALD HELLER'S PETITION FOR ORDER TO ARBITRATE AND FOR DISMISSAL OR STAY OF ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION. A R G U E D. COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT RULES AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT APPLIES TO "ANY AND ALL LEGAL DISPUTES" AND FEHA DISPUTES ARE ARBITRABLE. (ARMENDARIZ V. FOUNDATION HEALTH PSYCHCARE SERVICES, INC. (2000) 24 CAL.4TH 83, 93-96.) THE AGREEMENT'S EXCEPTION APPLIES TO ADMINISTRATIVE DFEH COMPLAINTS, NOT FEHA SUITS. (SEE ROMAN V. SUP. CT. (2009) 172 CAL.APP.4TH 1462, 1475-76.) THE CLAIMS AGAINST PROVOST HELLER ARE ARBITRABLE BECAUSE HE WAS THE AGENT THROUGH WHOM THE UNIVERSITY ACTED. (GARCIA V. PEXCO, LLC (2017) 11 CAL.APP.5TH 782, 788.) COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH IS VERBATIM TO THE COURTS TENTATIVE RULING AND COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: LORETTA NAJERA, CSR #5153 EMAIL: LNAJERA@SFTC.ORG

Notice Of Hearing On Defendants Petition For Order To Arbitrate And For Dismissal Or Stay Of Action Pending Arbitration
Off Calendar May-17-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Rebecca Wildman-Tobriner, Esq., Pierce Shearer LLP, Telephone 650-843-1900, appearing for Plaintiff;
Michael J. Vartain, Esq., Vartain Law Group, Telephone 415-391-1155, appearing for Defendant USF et al;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO, DONALD HELLER'S Petition For Order To Arbitrate And For Dismissal Or Stay Of Action Pending Arbitration.

Defendants' petition to compel arbitration and to stay this action is granted. The arbitration agreement applies to "any and all legal disputes" and FEHA disputes are arbitrable. (Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychcare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83, 93-96.) The agreement's exception applies to administrative DFEH complaints, not FEHA suits. (See Roman v. Sup. Ct. (2009) 172 Cal.App.4th 1462, 1475-76.) The claims against Provost Heller are arbitrable because he was the agent through whom the university acted. (Garcia v. Pexco, LLC (2017) 11 Cal.App.5th 782, 788.)

Counsel for defendants is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which is verbatim to the Courts tentative ruling and complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Loretta Najera, CSR #5153 email: lnajera@sftc.org, Reported. (302/RBU)
